body: {"rationale":"All 8 rubric criteria met. Title is imperative, names subsystem and change. Description has all four required sections (PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE, PROPOSED FIX, NON-SCOPE) plus explicit PARENT+DEPS. Root cause cites six specific file:line ranges across four files. AC items 1–5 each name a distinct TestXxx function; item 6 provides a filtered go test -run invocation; items 7–8 are the required full-suite and lefthook gate lines. Labels carry phase:2, two area: tags, kind:refactor, and two cross-ref tags (adr:024, spec:FEAT-010). Parent ddx-31e07fdc and dep ddx-7cb3153d are both explicit. A cold agent can identify files to edit (execute_bead.go:26-37, execute_bead_land.go:1549-1623, workers.go:756-992, state_executions.go:23-182), write the named tests, and run the gate commands without operator clarification.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}

{"base_rev":"5903531ad3ba479fd5af8f7abbc25c10ad4d6bc3","classification":"regression","decode_warnings":[{"field":"suggested_amendments","warning":"expected array; dropped malformed field","raw_excerpt":"["Next attempt must begin by running the failing tests in isolation before editing: `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass|TestExecuteBeadGateBlocksLanding' -v -count=1` to confirm the regression is reproducible from the landed commit, then trace why gate results are 'skipped' instead of 'pass'/'fail' — suspect the LandDecision or GateResult structs were restructured without updating the field that the gate runner writes results into.","Run `cd cli && go test ./i...[truncated]"},{"field":"classification","warning":"unknown classification","raw_excerpt":"regression"},{"field":"recommended_action","warning":"unknown recommended_action","raw_excerpt":"reopen_retry"}],"detail":"landed locally; push failed: git push origin ce52fa711b1fe714ceb860801010d15cd74a88cb:refs/heads/main: ╭────────────────────────────────────╮
